questions.question: What is a triangle?
questions.correct_answer: A polygon with three sides and three angles
questions.explanation: It is the simplest polygon with three edges and vertices.

questions.question: How are triangles classified by sides?
questions.explanation: Equilateral: all sides equal; Isosceles: two sides equal; Scalene: all sides different.

questions.question: How are triangles classified by angles?
questions.explanation: Based on the measure of angles: all <90°, one =90°, one >90° respectively.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles in a triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in any triangle always equals 180°.

questions.question: State Pythagoras Theorem.
questions.correct_answer: In a right-angled triangle, the square of hypotenuse = sum of squares of other two sides
questions.explanation: c² = a² + b² for right-angled triangle with hypotenuse c.

questions.question: What is a median of a triangle?
questions.correct_answer: A line joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side
questions.explanation: It divides the opposite side into two equal parts.

questions.question: What is an altitude of a triangle?
questions.correct_answer: A perpendicular from a vertex to the opposite side or its extension
questions.explanation: It represents the height corresponding to the base.

questions.question: Define congruent triangles.
questions.correct_answer: Triangles that are identical in shape and size
questions.explanation: All corresponding sides and angles are equal.

questions.question: Name the criteria for triangle congruence.
questions.explanation: Side-Side-Side, Side-Angle-Side, Angle-Side-Angle, Right-angle-Hypotenuse-Side are standard criteria.

questions.question: What is the relation between sides and angles in a triangle?
questions.correct_answer: The greater side lies opposite the greater angle
questions.explanation: This helps in comparing sides or angles based on the triangle’s geometry.

notes.title: Triangle Basics
notes.content: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 angles; sum of angles = 180°.

notes.title: Types of Triangles by Sides
notes.content: Equilateral, isosceles, scalene triangles based on side lengths.

notes.title: Types of Triangles by Angles
notes.content: Acute, right, obtuse based on interior angles.

notes.title: Triangle Congruence
notes.content: SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS rules to determine congruence of triangles.

notes.title: Pythagoras Theorem
notes.content: In right-angled triangle, hypotenuse² = sum of squares of other two sides.

facts.fact: The sum of angles in any triangle is always 180°.
All medians of a triangle intersect at a point called centroid.
Right-angled triangle can use Pythagoras theorem for side calculations.
Congruent triangles have equal corresponding sides and angles.
The greater side in a triangle is opposite the greater angle.
